title: Public signal field overflow allows Solidity/circuit semantic mismatch
description: The IdentityRegistry contract accepts public signals as uint256 and passes them directly to verifier contracts. Circom operates over BN254's scalar field (r ≈ 2^254), but Solidity uint256 goes up to 2^256-1. Values in range [r, 2^256-1] are valid in Solidity but would wrap modulo r in the circuit, creating a semantic mismatch. Specifically, sessionNonce and currentTimestamp are user-supplied public inputs — an attacker could submit sessionNonce = n + r which the contract treats as a different nonce (bypassing usedNonces) but the circuit reduces to n. Add require(pubSignal < r) checks in IdentityRegistry for every public input before calling the verifier, matching the pattern already used inside snarkjs-generated verifiers for proof elements. Write a Certora rule proving no public signal can exceed the scalar field bound.
